name: Bitsy Baskets Quilt
description: Mini-basket blocks make up this charming wall quilt. Follow the diagrams to make this quilt, or choose your own combinations for the baskets and backgrounds. The appliquéd posies add a whimsical touch. The quilt features 84 unique basket blocks arranged on point with gray borders and optional purple flange.

pieces.specialInstructions: Cut in half diagonally to make 2 half-square triangles; one triangle is used for the block background, one is leftover for another block
For HST construction; pair with Basket squares. Cut 2 3/8 inch if you do not wish to trim later. Yields 4 HST; 3 used in block, 1 leftover
For HST construction; pair with Background squares. Cut 2 3/8 inch if you do not wish to trim later. Yields 4 HST; 3 used in block, 1 leftover
Cut in half diagonally to make 4 half-square triangles; 3 are used in the block, 1 is leftover for another Basket Block

fabrics.name: Green
fabrics.specialInstructions: Includes fat 1/8 for blocks plus fat 1/4 for leaves

fabrics.name: Fabric 24
fabrics.specialInstructions: Includes fat 1/8 for blocks plus 3.5 yards for backing

fabrics.name: Fabric 28
fabrics.specialInstructions: Includes fat 1/8 for blocks plus 3/8 yard for binding

instructions: Cut (1) square 5 3/8 inches, cut in half diagonally to make 2 half-square triangles (one is used, one is leftover)
Cut (2) squares 2 1/2 inches (or 2 3/8 inches) for HST construction
Cut (1) handle from template
Cut (2) squares 2 1/2 inches (or 2 3/8 inches) for HST construction
Cut (2) squares 2 3/8 inches, cut in half diagonally to make 4 half-square triangles (three are used, one is leftover)
Cut background patches for 9 blocks
Cut (24) leaves (number is optional)
Cut (2) top/bottom borders 2 inches x 48 1/2 inches
Cut (2) side borders 2 inches x 45 1/2 inches
Cut (5) squares 7 5/8 inches, cut in half diagonally twice to make 20 quarter-square triangles for setting
Cut (2) squares 7 1/4 inches, cut in half diagonally to make 4 half-square triangles for corners
Cut background patches for 11 blocks
Cut background patches for 10 blocks
Cut background patches for 9 blocks
Cut background patches for 7 blocks
Cut background patches for 19 blocks
Cut background patches for 19 blocks
Cut (5) strips 1 inch x WOF, pieced to make (4) flanges 1 inch x 48 1/2 inches
Cut patches for 4 Baskets
Cut (5) strips 2 1/2 inches x WOF for binding
Cut (2) lengths 30 inches x 58 inches, pieced to fit quilt top with overlap on all sides

Cut patches as needed for the number of baskets indicated in each color: 4 Baskets for Fabrics 3, 8, 13, 17, 24, 28, 31; 3 Baskets for Fabrics 1, 2, 4, 6, 10, 12, 20, 21, 26, 32, 34, 36; 2 Baskets for Fabrics 9, 14, 18, 23, 25, 33, 35; 1 Basket for Fabrics 15, 29
Cut (12) flowers from leftovers (number of flowers is optional)

assemblySteps: Make HST units: Draw diagonal on wrong side of lighter background square. Pair with colored square, stitch 1/4 inch from each side of line, cut apart, press toward darker fabric, trim to 2 inches. Make 4 identical HST per block pair (3 used, 1 leftover).
Prepare appliqué handles: Trace handles from template onto fusible webbing, fuse to basket fabric, cut out.
Appliqué handle to large background triangle (from 5 3/8 inch square), centering handle and aligning ends with raw edge. Stitch edges.

Assemble block: Arrange 3 HST and 3 small colored triangles (from 2 3/8 inch squares) in rows as shown in diagram. Join rows. Sew unit to long edge of large background triangle with handle. Make 84 blocks.
Optional: Appliqué 12 flowers with leaves to desired blocks (placement optional, refer to photograph).
Arrange 84 blocks in diagonal rows on point, adding quarter-square triangle setting pieces (Fabric 7) at each end of rows. Join into rows, then join rows.
Sew half-square corner triangles (Fabric 7) to each corner. Trim quilt edges leaving 1/4 inch seam allowance.
Sew shorter gray borders to sides, then longer borders to top and bottom.
Layer quilt with batting and backing, baste. Quilt in ditch around borders and patches, around appliqué, and as desired in background.
Optional flange: Fold Purple (30) strips in half lengthwise. Align raw edges with quilt edges, baste slightly under 1/4 inch. Place on all four sides.
Bind quilt using binding strips, sewing to front and turning to back.
